channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why farmers in California are backing a giant solar farm
item.description: Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land ...

item.title: Tariffs cost American shoppers. They're unlikely to get that money back
item.description: After the Supreme Court declared the emergency tariffs illegal, the refund process will be messy and will go to businesses first.

item.title: Pentagon shifts toward maintaining ties to Scouting
item.description: Months after NPR reported on the Pentagon's efforts to sever ties with Scouting America, efforts to maintain the partnership have new momentum

item.title: Every business wants your review. What's with the feedback frenzy?
item.description: Customers want to read reviews and businesses need reviews to attract customers. But the constant demand for reviews could be creating a feedback back...

item.title: U.S. and Iran to hold nuclear talks. And, Harvard professor resigns over Epstein ties
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials are set to meet today in Geneva to discuss Tehran's nuclear program. And, Harvard professor Larry Summers is resigning over...

item.title: Is Ethiopia heading back to war in Tigray?
item.description: Many people are again leaving the region of Tigray just over three years after the civil war there ended.

item.title: US justice department accused of withholding Trump-related Epstein files
item.description: The top Democrat on the congressional panel investigating Epstein has called on the attorney general to publish the material.

item.title: 2/25: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump touts economy, immigration policies in State of the Union address; Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger hits Trump on affordability in Dem...

item.title: Russia says it has handed over 1,000 Ukrainian soldiers' bodies
item.description: Russia's announcement comes as Ukraine's top negotiator was meeting US peace envoys in Geneva.

item.title: Most Americans see Iran as an enemy but doubt Trump’s judgment on military force, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: A new AP-NORC poll finds that as the U.S. and Iran head into their next round of nuclear talks in Geneva, many U.S. adults continue to view Iran’s nuc...

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Can the U.S. and Iran reach a nuclear deal to avert a war?
item.description: Trump says he won't let Iran to build a nuclear weapon, and Iran says it doesn't intend to, but as talks resume, experts see war as more likely than a...

item.title: Memorial services for Jesse Jackson begin at Chicago headquarters of his civil rights organization
Singer D4vd confirmed as 'target' of investigation into murder of teen
item.description: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

item.title: Girl, 14, shot dead as South Africa's 'taxi wars' hit school
item.description: Violence has plagued South Africa's highly competitive and lucrative taxi industry for decades.

item.title: 2/25: CBS Evening News
item.description: Four killed aboard U.S. speedboat that entered Cuban waters and opened fire, Cuba says; Moving crew helps rescue abducted toddler by blocking truck.

item.title: Instagram to alert parents when teens search for info on suicide
item.description: Starting next week, parents will get an alert if their teen repeatedly searches for certain terms related to self-harm or suicide in a short time span...

item.title: What to know about the LA superintendent whose home was searched by FBI
